serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Simply Vegetarian is approximately 250 g, as typically served at a Singaporean hawker centre or food court.

nutrient_source.title: Simply Vegetarian (D5) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'D5. Simply Vegetarian' (cr_id G00010800) with confidence 0.95. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed vegetable and tofu dishes, analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Singaporean mixed-vegetable tofu vermicelli dish. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous mixed-vegetable stir-fry dishes with tofu and glass/rice vermicelli from the University of Sydney GI Database. The low carbohydrate content (3.12 g/100g), high vegetable fibre, and tofu protein all attenuate glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as: GI (35) × carbohydrate per serving (7.8 g from 250 g portion) / 100 = 2.7.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 144 mg/100g with favourable potassium ratio (114 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 144 mg/100g is well below the 200 mg/100g threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods, and the potassium content of 114 mg/100g supports a favourable Na:K ratio consistent with DASH dietary principles; patients should nonetheless request reduced-sodium soy sauce during preparation to maintain this low-sodium profile.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (3.12 g/100g), low sugar (1.61 g/100g), estimated low GI (~35)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The very low carbohydrate content (3.12 g/100g), low sugar (1.61 g/100g), estimated low glycemic index (~35), and dietary fibre from mixed vegetables collectively support glycemic stability; patients with Type 2 Diabetes may consume this dish freely but should verify that no sweet sauces or added sugars are used in preparation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~0.3 g/100g), low energy density (33 kcal/100g), vegetable-dominant profile
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low saturated fat content (~0.3 g/100g), very low energy density (33 kcal/100g), and vegetable-dominant composition with tofu isoflavones make this dish cardioprotective; the modest cholesterol of 6 mg/100g is clinically insignificant, and the dish aligns well with heart-healthy dietary patterns for cardiovascular disease management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory vegetable-dominant profile with low saturated fat
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The vegetable-dominant composition, low saturated fat, and likely presence of anti-inflammatory polyphenols from cruciferous vegetables, garlic, and ginger support an anti-inflammatory dietary pattern suitable for rheumatoid arthritis; nightshade-sensitive patients should request omission of bell peppers or tomatoes if included.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Simply Vegetarian (D5) is a Singaporean hawker-style dish consisting of a medley of fresh mixed vegetables, firm tofu, mushrooms, and glass vermicelli (bean thread noodles) cooked in a light, savoury sauce typically made with soy sauce, garlic, and ginger. The dish is stir-fried or gently braised, resulting in tender vegetables and silken-to-firm tofu pieces with a mild umami flavour and soft, slightly chewy texture from the vermicelli. It is commonly served as a side dish or light main at Singaporean food courts and hawker centres, particularly popular among vegetarian diners. Nutritionally, it is very low in energy (33 kcal/100g), low in carbohydrates (3.12 g/100g), and provides modest protein (0.67 g/100g) from tofu and dietary fibre (1.1 g/100g) from vegetables. Sodium is 144 mg/100g, which is acceptable for hypertension management. The dish has an estimated low glycemic index (~35) and is suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. It is vegetarian and likely halal, though soy sauce may contain gluten. The cholesterol content of 6 mg/100g may indicate a small egg or dairy component; consumers with strict dietary requirements should verify ingredients with the vendor.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010800) with a recorded confidence of 0.95 and review status 'reviewed'. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singaporean dishes. GI is an analogous estimate (low confidence for GI specifically) but all core macros and secondary nutrients are exact HPB values.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific Singaporean mixed-vegetable tofu dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (G00010800) as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat estimated at 0.3 g/100g based on the low total fat (2.15 g/100g) and vegetable-oil cooking medium typical of this dish category in HPB data. Cholesterol at 6 mg/100g suggests a minor egg or dairy component may be present; consumers with strict vegetarian or vegan requirements should verify with the vendor. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable mixed-vegetable tofu stir-fry dishes; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found in approved sources. is_vegan set to false due to the presence of cholesterol (6 mg/100g) suggesting possible non-vegan ingredient.
